title: Denis Beau: Stablecoins - what strategic choices for Europe?
summary: Denis Beau, First Deputy Governor of the Bank of France, outlined Europe's strategic response to stablecoins at the EUROFI High Level Seminar on March 26, 2026. He emphasized the need to preserve the two-tier monetary system while developing tokenized finance, advocating for a triple objective: adapting central bank money services, supporting tokenized private money from European institutions, and designing adequate regulation. Beau highlighted that MiCA is the world's first comprehensive crypto regulatory framework but requires strengthening, particularly regarding stablecoin use for everyday payments and multi-issuance regulation.
key_points: Stablecoins are currently predominantly USD-denominated and largely issued by non-EU players, creating 'stablecoinisation' and 'dollarisation' risks for European payment systems
Banque de France is advancing three projects (Pontes, Appia, digital euro) and will deploy wholesale central bank money services in tokenized form by end of 2026
MiCA is the first comprehensive crypto-asset regulatory framework globally, but only partially addresses risks from widespread adoption of non-European stablecoins
Beau advocates strengthening MiCA to restrict use of non-euro stablecoins for everyday payments and impose stricter regulation on multi-issuance of stablecoins
Stablecoins issued by banks or EMIs within banking groups present structurally lower counterparty risk than non-bank issuers due to central bank liquidity access
Full, timely, consistent, and global implementation of FSB Standards on crypto-assets is needed based on 'same activities, same risks, same rules' principle
European Commission released Market Integration Package in December 2025 to remove regulatory barriers to DLT and amend DLT Pilot Regulation
why_it_matters: This speech provides insight into Europe's approach to stablecoin regulation and digital currency infrastructure, which could affect how crypto assets are issued, used, and regulated in the EU. Market participants dealing with stablecoins, tokenized assets, or European payment systems should understand these regulatory priorities as implementation advances.
market_structure_context: The speech directly addresses market structure concerns around payment systems and settlement infrastructure. Key points include: (1) preservation of the two-tier monetary system (central bank money + private money) as foundation for tokenized finance; (2) need for secure, efficient, pan-European public and private payment solutions; (3) coexistence, complementarity, and substitutability at par between public and regulated private money; (4) non-bank stablecoin issuers cannot currently access central bank accounts under Eurosystem policy; (5) potential future access for non-bank issuers that provide payment services under certain conditions.
notes: This speech is recent (published April 9, 2026) and provides substantive policy perspectives on European stablecoin regulation and payment infrastructure. While classified as speech_neutral due to no directional signal on interest rates or monetary policy, it is highly relevant to market structure. The speaker is a central bank official (First Deputy Governor of Bank of France) speaking in an official capacity, though the disclaimer notes views are those of the speaker. The speech contains forward-looking commitments (wholesale tokenized central bank money deployment by end of 2026) and concrete regulatory recommendations (strengthening MiCA).
